# .updated-plan.md Format

This file defines the format contract between Interrogator and Planner.
It lives at the workspace root. Interrogator writes it from a ticket or
plain-language description; Planner reads it (or accepts its content as
injected context) to fold edge cases and open questions into
.tdd-plan.md.

## Sections (written by Interrogator)

\`\`\`markdown
<!-- generated by Interrogator on YYYY-MM-DD -->

## Source
[ticket id or "prompt"] - [one-line summary]

## Edge Cases
- [edge case or error condition implied by the requirements]
(or "None found")

## Open Questions
- [ambiguity or gap that a human should resolve before/while planning]
(or "None")
\`\`\`

## Rules

- Interrogator only documents — it never writes test or production code,
  and never writes .tdd-plan.md.
- Edge cases must be concrete and testable (a Tester agent should be able
  to turn each one into a test), not generic categories like "handle
  errors".
- Open Questions are for genuine ambiguities the ticket doesn't resolve
  (e.g. conflicting requirements, undefined behaviour for a stated case).
  Don't pad this list to seem thorough.
- This file is overwritten by Interrogator on each new pass - it reflects
  the current ticket/task, not a running history.
- Planner treats ## Edge Cases from .updated-plan.md as input to its own
  ## Edge Cases section (merge, don't duplicate). Planner treats
  ## Open Questions as a signal: unresolved questions about explicit
  acceptance criteria are grounds to fail fast rather than guess.

## Context Injection

In orchestrated environments, Planner may receive .updated-plan.md content
as injected context in the prompt rather than reading the file directly,
the same way it receives ticket content. The file on disk remains the
canonical persistence layer for human reference and non-orchestrated use.
